title: Credentials, Signing, Retries, Timeouts, and Client Resilience · Go Semantics Case: Retry Classification Budget
summary: A process accepts static keys from request data, retries every failure beyond its caller deadline, and records neither attempt identity nor ambiguous write state. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retry classification budget increment in bounded S3 request and retry state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Classify throttling, transient transport, server, permanent, cancellation, conflict, and ambiguous write outcomes with bounded attempts, time, delay, and bytes.
Compose request, attempt, transfer, and shutdown deadlines and propagate cancellation through body and goroutine ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Retry Classification Budget · Theory Retry Classification Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6787 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify S3 outcomes and bound retries while preserving write ambiguity; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify throttling, transient transport, server, permanent, cancellation, conflict, and ambiguous write outcomes with bounded attempts, time, delay, and bytes.
